mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor demand and future earnings deterioration. A month-over-month contraction in PAYEMS predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations compress in anticipation of lower corporate profits.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2011 payroll growth was weak at 54k jobs
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with fragile labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where PAYEMS declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) indicates an extended monetary accommodation regime. Under this regime, equity risk premia remain compressed and equity valuations are supported by the absence of competing safe-asset yields, producing persistently positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% in May 2011, near zero-bound
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in zero-rate periods (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) are not significantly higher than periods with FEDFUNDS > 1%, or Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates consumer spending strength decoupled from housing investment. This divergence signals demand rotation away from capital-intensive sectors, reducing near-term equity market breadth and producing negative 5-day returns as sector rotation creates temporary volatility.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month decline > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 523k in April 2011, declining trend
regime_hint: demand composition shift in post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: The interaction signal (RSAFS rising while HOUST falling) does not produce negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below its 3-month moving average, signaling weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side constraints. This supply weakness, absent demand destruction, produces positive equity returns as investors anticipate pricing power and margin expansion over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth falls below its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 92.98 in May 2011, post-crisis recovery phase
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO deceleration are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal produces negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 9% signals persistent slack in labor markets and downward wage pressure. Under this regime, corporate profit margins expand despite weak revenue growth, supporting equity valuations over a 60-day horizon as cost pressures remain muted.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% in May 2011, elevated post-crisis level
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in high-unemployment periods (UNRATE > 9%) are not significantly higher than low-unemployment periods (UNRATE < 5%), or the effect reverses in the most recent 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above its 12-month trend signals monetary policy lag and future rate-hiking expectations. This inflation acceleration produces negative 20-day equity returns as investors reprice discount rates and expect Fed tightening to slow growth.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 224.80 in May 2011, modest inflation backdrop
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following CPI acceleration (MoM > 0.3%) are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal produces positive returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates demand growth concentrated in non-residential sectors. This composition shift reduces equity market exposure to housing-sensitive sectors, producing negative 5-day returns as investors rotate away from construction and materials stocks.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND HOUST month-over-month decline > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13441.9B in Q1 2011, HOUST declining
failure_condition: The interaction signal does not produce negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint effect is weaker than either component alone (interaction coefficient not significant at p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) both declining month-over-month signals synchronized supply and labor weakness. This dual contraction predicts negative 20-day equity returns as investors recognize demand destruction and earnings deterioration across the economy.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline AND PAYEMS month-over-month decline
evidence_refs: fact:Both series showed weakness in May 2011 post-shock period
regime_hint: synchronized economic contraction or shock period
failure_condition: The interaction signal (both INDPRO and PAYEMS declining) does not produce negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a logistic regression model.
